title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Problem Details
summary: A package-signing client must distinguish protocol failure, rejected policy, malformed problem details, and an unknown write outcome. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go problem details increment in a Go 1.26 net/http typed response and failure classifier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse RFC 9457 problem details by type and extensions without scraping human-readable title or detail text. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
